item.title: SQLite Query Explainer 摘要
item.description:
Simon Willison built an interactive SQLite query explainer tool that runs SQLite in Python via Pyodide in Web Assembly in the browser. Inspired by Julia Evans' admission of difficulty reading query plans, the tool adds explanatory layers to EXPLAIN and EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN results. Willison cautions that he cannot fully verify the explanations himself but finds them plausible. The tool is available on his website and was built using Fable.
标签: sqlite, sql, tools, query-plan, pyodide, webassembly, browser, julia-evans, simon-willison, fable

item.title: Make It Work vs. Make It Good 摘要
item.description:
The author describes an internal conflict between wanting to be a designer and a developer. Making something 'work' is easier and often receives more praise for its novelty, while making something 'good' requires nuanced, multi-disciplinary effort that is often met with ambivalence because good design appears obvious in hindsight. The article uses the example of making a website responsive: getting it to work is simple and applauded, but making it a truly good experience across devices, accessibility, and performance is much harder. The author concludes that 'make it work' is the first 90% of the work, and 'make it good' is the other 90%.
标签: design, development, craft, quality, user experience, web development, responsive design, perception, effort, taste

item.title: What's the deal with all the random weekly quota resets for agents lately? 摘要
item.description:
Subscription-based coding agents like Claude Code and Codex impose 5-hour and weekly quotas to prevent server overload and discourage binge usage. Recently, Anthropic and OpenAI have been frequently resetting weekly quotas, especially after releasing Fable 5 and GPT-5.6 Sol. OpenAI reset Codex quotas six times in two weeks, plus banked resets. While intended as a gift, these unpredictable resets cause annoyance for power users who try to fully utilize their quota, leading to wasted quota and disrupted plans. The surge may aim to prevent users from switching to competitors like Grok 4.5 or Muse Spark 1.1. However, frequent resets could devalue quotas and incentivize downgrading plans.
标签: coding agents, quota resets, Claude Code, Codex, OpenAI, Anthropic, GPT-5.6, Fable 5, LLM competition, power users

item.title: Apple Sends Letters to Dozens of Former Employees Now at OpenAI 摘要
item.description:
Apple has sent personal legal letters to about 40 former employees now working at OpenAI, demanding they preserve documents and meet with Apple's lawyers. This aggressive tactic follows Apple's recent lawsuit accusing OpenAI and two employees of stealing secret hardware plans. Apple and OpenAI declined to comment. The move highlights Apple's aggressive legal strategy in protecting its trade secrets.
标签: Apple, OpenAI, lawsuit, trade secrets, legal, hardware, employees, tech, corporate espionage, litigation
